title: waltheria coriacea
intro: Waltheria coriacea, also known as the Indian walnut or Coriacea, is a plant species native to tropical Africa and Asia, characterized by its distinctive walnut-like fruit. The leaves and roots of Waltheria coriacea have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wounds and skin conditions. Waltheria coriacea is typically prepared in herbal teas or infusions, and its extracts have also been used in traditional medicine to make tonics, ointments, and other medicinal preparations.
